label: Agent Spawn
description: Spawn a single agent through openagentic-proxy. The proxy resolves the agent role to a DB-backed agent record and runs the task.

settings.name: taskDescription
settings.label: Task description (legacy alias)
settings.description: Legacy alias for task — used when 'task' is unset.

settings.name: agentType
settings.label: Agent type
settings.description: High-level role hint (e.g. researcher, coder, analyst). Mapped to a DB agent_type by the executor.

settings.name: agentRole
settings.label: Agent role override
settings.description: Explicit role (overrides agentType). Useful when the calling template knows the exact DB agent_type.

settings.name: agentId
settings.label: Agent ID
settings.description: Pin the call to a specific DB agent by id. Bypasses role-based resolution.

settings.name: model
settings.label: Model
settings.description: Leave as 'auto' to let openagentic-proxy / Smart Router pick the model.

settings.name: tools
settings.label: Tools
settings.description: Optional list of MCP tool names the agent is allowed to invoke.

ai.whenToUse: When you need a single specialized agent (researcher, coder, analyst) to take a task and return a result.
ai.examplePrompt: spawn a researcher agent to summarize the latest news on the input topic
ai.promptHints: Reference the result with {{steps.<id>.content}}. Leave model as 'auto' so openagentic-proxy + Smart Router pick the right model.

outputAssertions.name: agent_returned_content
outputAssertions.errorMessage: Agent did not return any content.

outputAssertions.name: agent_substantive_output
outputAssertions.expression: !result || !result.content || !result.content.match(/^\s*(I (could not|couldn't|cannot|can't|do not have|don't have|am unable|wasn't able|was not able)|Sorry,? (I|but)|I'?m sorry,?|I apologize,?|Unfortunately,? (I|no)|No (information|data|results|content) (available|found))/i)
outputAssertions.errorMessage: Agent returned a refusal or 'no info' response — likely the underlying tool returned no results.
